guest: Jeremy Giffon, first employee at Tiny, Founders Fund partner

business_ideas.idea: Build businesses for content creators and audiences as co-founders
Wirecutter-style guide for non-toxic consumer products and homes
Regulatory compliance software for specific jurisdictions and industries
Distressed venture capital targeting misaligned cap tables

predictions.text: Audiences are underpriced and will be primary moat for consumer businesses
Regulation only increases, creating durable competitive advantages

dynamics: Giffon presents multiple business opportunity theses. Sam and others ask clarifying questions. Discussion covers Tiny's strategy of acquiring and improving businesses while exploring new models.
frameworks: Audience co-founder model - creator owns equity, operator runs business
Special situations investing - fixing broken cap tables, not broken businesses
Regulation as moat - increasing compliance requirements create defensibility
One-chart businesses - simple visualization of value thesis drives conviction
learnings: Audiences built through content are valuable moats not fully monetized yet
Creators + operators partnership more efficient than creator solo-building
Many venture-backed companies are broken due to cap table issues, not business model problems
Unglamorous industries offer better economics and less competition
Simplifying complex businesses often more valuable than building from scratch

banger_quotes.quote: Audiences are underpriced, still very early on monetization
Regulation only goes in one direction - up and to the right
Everyone with an audience will eventually have tightly integrated organic products

numbers_dropped: Tiny: turned $5M equity into ~$500M business value through acquisitions
Onnit (Joe Rogan): sold for significant amount, major audience monetization example
Cars and Bids (Doug DeMuro): successful audience-backed business
Prime energy drink (Logan Paul/KSI): targeting $10M+ revenue
Matina yerba mate (Huberman partnership): audience-driven co-branding
notable: Part 2 continuation of Giffon discussion; focus on identifying broken but valuable businesses and audience monetization opportunities
